title: Construction operatives n.e.c.
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate, usually through subordinate supervisory personnel, activities concerned with the construction and maintenance of structures, facilities, and systems. Participate in the conceptual development of a construction project and oversee its organization, scheduling, budgeting, and implementation. Includes managers in specialized construction fields, such as carpentry or plumbing. Apply knowledge of engineering, biology, chemistry, computer science, and biomechanical principles to the design, development, and evaluation of biological, agricultural, and health systems and products, such as artificial organs, prostheses, instrumentation, medical information systems, and health management and care delivery systems. Perform engineering duties in planning, designing, and overseeing construction and maintenance of building structures and facilities, such as roads, railroads, airports, bridges, harbors, channels, dams, irrigation projects, pipelines, power plants, and water and sewage systems. (Based on 78 related US occupations.)

work_activities.element_name: Communicating with Supervisors, Peers, or Subordinates
Operating Vehicles, Mechanized Devices, or Equipment
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards
Coordinating the Work and Activities of Others
Estimating the Quantifiable Characteristics of Products, Events, or Information
Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
Communicating with People Outside the Organization
Interpreting the Meaning of Information for Others
Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
Drafting, Laying Out, and Specifying Technical Devices, Parts, and Equipment
Providing Consultation and Advice to Others
Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

Work With or Contribute to a Work Group or Team
Deal With External Customers or the Public in General
Coordinate or Lead Others in Accomplishing Work Activities
Health and Safety of Other Workers
Work Outcomes and Results of Other Workers
Dealing With Unpleasant, Angry, or Discourteous People
Dealing with Violent or Physically Aggressive People
Outdoors, Exposed to All Weather Conditions

Spend Time Kneeling, Crouching, Stooping, or Crawling
Spend Time Keeping or Regaining Balance

Spend Time Bending or Twisting Your Body
Wear Common Protective or Safety Equipment such as Safety Shoes, Glasses, Gloves, Hearing Protection, Hard Hats, or Life Jackets
Wear Specialized Protective or Safety Equipment such as Breathing Apparatus, Safety Harness, Full Protection Suits, or Radiation Protection
Impact of Decisions on Co-workers or Company Results
Importance of Being Exact or Accurate
Pace Determined by Speed of Equipment

tasks.task: Install, repair, or replace roofing systems using waterproof materials, including applying alternate layers of asphalt or tar and roofing paper, and smoothing surfaces for waterproofing.

Cover roofs or exterior walls with materials such as slate, asphalt, or aluminum using various tools; install vapor barriers and insulation on flat roofs; and attach roofing paper in overlapping strips.
Inspect roofs for repairs, estimate materials and labor needed, and clean job sites before, during, and after roofing applications.
Set up scaffolding and provide safe access to roofs, as well as remove snow, water, or debris prior to roofing work.
Direct rig crews, monitor drilling operations, and maintain and adjust machinery to ensure proper performance during construction and maintenance activities.
Repair or replace machinery parts, including pumps and rotary drill rigs, while ensuring proper functionality and documenting work performed.
Prepare surfaces for plastering, cementing, or similar applications by cleaning, smoothing, and applying suitable adhesives or compounds.
Mix and apply sealing compounds and adhesives to wall and floor materials, ensuring proper fitting and smooth finishes; manage adhesive selection and joint sealing.
Install insulation in building exteriors and handle wallboard assembly, ensuring correct installation according to guidelines and preparing surfaces for finishing.
Guide machinery used in construction and clean or prepare job sites and surfaces; supervise crew members in their tasks; report on project progress and conditions.
Monitor equipment for leaks and troubleshoot issues with drilling fluids and drilling operations to optimize performance.
Install drainage, insulation, or landscaping features as part of the construction process and manage tools or materials associated with roofing, drilling, or installation.
Prepare and handle site conditions while ensuring safety measures, such as closing or maintaining wells and conducting safety tests.
Participate in construction project development by providing estimates, conducting site assessments, and coordinating activities with specialized contractors.

Install guide wires on exterior surfaces of buildings to indicate the thickness of plaster or stucco, and nail wire mesh, lath, or similar materials to hold stucco in place. Apply weatherproof, decorative coverings to exterior surfaces of buildings, such as troweling or spraying on coats of stucco. Measure and cut openings in panels or tiles for electrical outlets, windows, vents, plumbing, or other fixtures using keyhole saws or other cutting tools. Cut and screw together metal channels to make floor or ceiling frames according to plans for the location of rooms or hallways. Hang drywall panels on metal frameworks, using lifts or hoists to adjust panel heights when necessary. Steady pipes during connection to or disconnection from drill or casing strings. Fit and fasten wallboard or drywall into position on wood or metal frameworks. Coordinate work with drywall finishers who cover seams between drywall panels. Prepare surfaces for tiling and layout work according to blueprints or drawings. Measure, cut, and apply tile and carpet using various tools and techniques. Clean construction sites and prepare surfaces prior to installation. Read blueprints and specifications to prepare for work activities.

Perform routine maintenance on vehicles and equipment and maintain safety inspections. Lubricate, clean, or repair machinery, equipment, or tools. Operate mechanized equipment, such as pickup trucks, rollers, or backhoes. Drive truck-mounted units to well sites. Monitor control panels during pumping operations, ensuring materials are being pumped correctly. Assist craft workers such as carpenters, plasterers, or masons. Supervise workers engaged in producing oil from wells. Prepare cost and labor estimates based on calculations of time and materials needed for construction projects. Provide supervision in laying pipe and control traffic near work zones.

Mix mortar and plaster to desired consistency and apply for walls, floors, and ceilings. Lay and set mosaic tiles and finish joints, ensuring even coverage and a professional appearance. Install carpet, including padding and tack strips, and stretch carpet for a smooth finish. Lay gas and oil pipelines and install fixtures in designated positions with hand tools. Cut and shape tile to fit around obstacles and prepare surfaces for application, including insulation work. Set stone or marble in place and smooth, polish, and bevel surfaces as required. Dig trenches or foundations for installations. Ensure alignment and proper slopes using levels or lasers. Measure and cut insulation for various applications and install insulation materials as needed.

Drill holes in timbers or lumber and cut them to specified dimensions. Lay out pipe routes according to written instructions or blueprints and coordinate with supervisors. Repair cracked or damaged materials and inspect surfaces to determine conditions prior to covering.

Observe and report hazardous conditions, ensuring compliance with safety protocols at construction sites. Clean up pre- and post-installation areas, including vacuuming carpets and disposing of remnants. Determine methods of installation based on building codes and specifications, and update tags or labels on components as needed.

Remove old materials, including insulation and tiles, and conduct repairs on existing structures. Supervise or train workers in complex tasks, ensuring adherence to safety and procedural guidelines. Control traffic passing near or in work zones for safety purposes. Erect or dismantle temporary structures such as scaffolding as needed.
Spray acoustic materials or texture finish over walls or ceilings. Unload and assemble pipes and pumping equipment, using hand tools. Supervise oil pumpers and manage the installation of systems.

Build underbeds and install necessary components for structural integrity in construction projects. Prepare trucks and equipment necessary for specific on-site tasks and assist in operational tasks within project timelines.

Remove sections of materials from structures and assist in the completion of tasks for renovation purposes. Implement safety and cleaning procedures while maintaining quality in construction supervision.
Not applicable to the construction operatives role and considered irrelevant to main activities. This task will be removed from the task list.
Tend machines that pump concrete, grout, cement, sand, plaster, or stucco through spray guns for application to ceilings or walls, and mix, pour, or spread concrete using portable cement mixers.
Grind, scrape, sand, or polish surfaces, such as concrete, marble, or wood flooring, using abrasive tools or machines.
Place, consolidate, or protect case-in-place concrete or masonry structures, and install sewer, water, or storm drain pipes using pipe-laying machinery or laser guidance equipment.
Mop, brush, or spread paints, cleaning solutions, or other compounds over surfaces to clean them or provide protection.
Tend pumps, compressors, or generators to provide power for tools or to heat or move materials, and perform rough-ins, repair and replace fixtures, and locate or repair leaking pipes.
Anchor steel supports from ceiling joists to hold pipes in place, and connect water heaters and storage tanks to power and water sources.
Erect scaffolding and supply or hold tools and materials for construction activities.
Apply weather seal, such as pipe flashings and sealants, to roof penetrations, and check to ensure that completed roofs are watertight.
Install and maintain plumbing systems, including circulating pumps, copper or plastic plumbing, and fixtures, according to specifications.
Install solar subsystem components, including mounting devices for solar collectors and supporting structures, in accordance with design and manufacturer specifications.
Install and service electrical distribution and transmission systems, including conduits and related equipment, complying with safety and installation standards.
Transport tools, materials, equipment, and supplies to work sites and assist in the preparation, installation, repair, or rebuilding of surfaces.
Maintain tools and equipment, clean work areas, and adhere to safety practices and procedures during construction activities.
Cut, thread, or assemble pipes and fittings, including modifications for routing through walls or ceilings, and inspect plumbing and piping systems for defects or hazards.
Measure, cut, and assemble new piping systems, ensuring proper installation and adherence to building codes and specifications.
Demonstrate and assist in various construction duties, including cleaning and maintaining work areas and adhering to safety procedures.
Clean and maintain work areas, machines, and tools to ensure a safe and efficient job site.
Position and hold materials such as timbers, lumber, or panels in place for construction, and align, straighten, or square forms as needed for installation.
Select tools, equipment, or materials from storage and transport them to work sites, and requisition tools or equipment as necessary.
Install and repair structures and systems, including but not limited to plumbing, electrical, and mechanical systems, and maintain or repair existing systems by replacing faulty components.
Construct forms for concrete, spread, level, and smooth concrete, and ensure proper curing by monitoring environmental conditions.
Assemble and install fences, including measuring and laying out lines, digging postholes, and securing posts with concrete or soil.
Installs fixtures such as handrails, windows, and doors, and ensure they function properly by testing operation and making adjustments as needed.
Perform tests for leaks and repairs on pipelines and irrigation systems, and install controls and fittings to enhance system functionality.
Discuss project requirements with clients or team members, including estimating costs, determining materials needed, and scheduling work procedures.
Supervise and direct work activities of helpers or other laborers in construction and maintenance tasks to ensure projects are completed efficiently.
Install, maintain, and repair HVAC systems, including air ducts and controls, to optimize heating and cooling efficiency.
Monitor and test equipment operations to identify any malfunctions, perform diagnostics, and execute necessary repairs or adjustments.
Manage site cleanliness and safety, including removing contaminated materials and applying protective measures against hazardous materials.
Help in the conceptual development of construction projects and participate in overseeing scheduling and implementation processes.
Perform basic laboring duties, including drilling, cutting, and assembling materials using hand and power tools.
Apply insulation and weatherization materials to reduce energy losses and improve efficiency in buildings.
Inspect work to verify that structures comply with specifications and safety regulations, and conduct preventative maintenance on equipment.
Report on and document progress of construction tasks and any encountered issues during the process.
Install automatic controls and valves in plumbing and mechanical systems to optimize system performance.

Plan, organize, or direct activities concerned with the construction or maintenance of structures, facilities, or systems, including estimating costs, preparing budget estimates, and negotiating contracts.
Inspect or review projects to monitor compliance with building and safety codes, environmental regulations, and to ensure responsible design and construction practices.
Perform general maintenance, repair, and installation of machinery, equipment, tools, electrical components, and structures using various hand and power tools.
Train or manage maintenance personnel or construction workers, and conduct safety checks on equipment before operation.
Diagnose equipment and machinery malfunctions, and perform routine maintenance to ensure optimal performance of construction equipment.
Maintain inventory levels, requisition supplies or materials, and order new parts or equipment from suppliers as necessary.
Design, fabricate, and repair structures or components, including wooden structures, metal parts, or specialized equipment.
Develop or implement quality control programs and environmental protection programs to minimize impacts associated with construction activities.
Compile and keep accurate records of maintenance and repair work, and ensure compliance with regulations and safety procedures.
Evaluate construction methods and implement new or modified plans in response to delays or emergencies at construction sites.
Communicate and confer with supervisory personnel, clients, or contractors to discuss work procedures and resolve construction issues.
Piece and install various materials, ensure site cleanliness, and manage proper disposal of waste materials.
Prepare hazardous materials for removal or storage, and perform activities related to environmental management and safety.
Assemble and position equipment, including cranes and trucks for construction activities, and responsibly manage their operation.
Conduct safety checks and assess drilling conditions when working with explosives or hazardous materials.
Remove hazardous materials such as asbestos or lead, ensuring safety protocols are adhered to throughout the process.
Manage construction timelines, assess labor requirements, and dispatch workers to various sites as necessary.
Analyze and select appropriate construction methods, designs, and materials based on project specifications.
Direct acquisition of land for construction projects and ensure all necessary permits are obtained prior to beginning work.
Withdraw drill rods from holes, extract core samples, and operate water-well drilling rigs and other equipment to drill, bore, and dig for water wells or for environmental assessment purposes.
Regulate air pressure, rotary speed, and downward pressure while drilling based on the type of rock or concrete.
Perform pumping tests to assess well performance and gather and analyze water use data to forecast water demand.
Assemble and position machines, augers, casing pipes, and retrieve lost equipment from bore holes using hand and power tools and retrieval tools.
Record drilling progress and geological data, and document geological formations encountered during work.
Drive trucks, tractors, or truck-mounted drills to and from work sites, and drive or guide truck-mounted equipment into position.
Perform routine maintenance and upgrade work on machines and equipment, including replacing parts and lubricating machinery.
Operate machines to flush earth cuttings or blow dust from holes, and start, stop, and control drilling speed of machines and insertion of casings into holes.
Verify depths and alignments of boring positions, select the appropriate drill for jobs based on rock or soil conditions, and design water or wastewater lift stations.
Conduct environmental impact studies related to water and wastewater collection, treatment, or distribution, and perform hydraulic analyses of water supply systems.
Oversee the construction of decentralized or on-site wastewater treatment systems, and design or select equipment for compliance with wastewater processing standards.
Analyze the efficiency of water delivery structures and conduct feasibility studies for construction of water supply systems or wastewater collection systems.
Analyze and recommend chemical, biological, or other wastewater treatment methods, and analyze storm water or floodplain drainage systems.
Develop plans for new water resources, design pumping systems and pipelines for wastewater collection, and identify design alternatives for new water resources.
Participate in contract bidding, negotiation, or administration, and supervise the maintenance or repair of transportation systems or components.
Prepare or present public reports on topics such as bid proposals, environmental impact statements, or property descriptions, and provide technical advice regarding construction modifications.
Inspect project sites to monitor progress and ensure compliance with design specifications and environmental regulations.
Evaluate transportation systems or traffic control devices to determine needs for modification, and investigate traffic problems to recommend methods to improve flow or safety.
Review development plans to determine potential traffic impacts and design or prepare plans for new transportation systems or parts of systems.
Estimate quantities and costs of materials, and prepare project budgets, schedules, or specifications for labor or materials.
Check construction plans and design calculations for conformity to engineering standards, and analyze environmental impact statements for transportation projects.
Evaluate construction project materials for compliance with environmental standards, and incorporate sustainable materials or products into transportation structures.
Direct engineering activities and ensure compliance with environmental, safety, or governmental regulations, and manage construction, operations, or maintenance activities at project sites.
Perform mathematical modeling of water resources and investigate or test specific construction project materials for compliance with specifications or standards.
Install photovoltaic (PV) systems according to codes and perform routine maintenance on PV systems including testing and cleaning modules, as well as ensuring safety checks.
Develop or implement engineering solutions for cleaning up contaminated sites, and provide technical support for water resource or treatment issues.

Inspect completed transportation projects to ensure safety or compliance with applicable standards or regulations, and conduct studies of traffic patterns or environmental conditions to identify engineering problems.
Direct other workers to move stakes, place blocks or position anchors or cables.
Test soils or materials to determine adequacy and strength of foundations or construction materials.
Maintain records of materials moved, and compile or maintain records of system operation, performance, and maintenance.
Monitor loading processes to ensure materials are loaded according to specifications, and measure levels of excavated material to prevent equipment overloads.
Clean, service, and perform safety checks on equipment and repair or replace parts as necessary.
Pry off loose material from roofs and move it into paths of machines using crowbars, and measure and analyze system performance to assess operational conditions of systems or equipment.
Replace hydraulic hoses, headlight bulbs, and gathering-arm teeth, and perform manual labor to prepare or finish sites by shoveling materials by hand.
Handle slides, mud, or pit cleanings or maintenance, and drive machines to work sites.
Adjust dig face angles for varying overburden depths and set lengths, and create or maintain inclines or ramps.

Direct workers engaged in placing blocks or outriggers to prevent capsizing of machines when lifting heavy loads, and direct ground workers engaged in activities such as moving stakes or markers or changing positions of towers.

Receive written or oral instructions regarding material movement or excavation, and observe hand signals, grade stakes, or other markings when operating machines to ensure work is performed to specifications.
Move materials over short distances around a construction site, factory, or warehouse.
Lubricate, adjust, or repair machinery and replace parts such as gears, bearings, or bucket teeth.
Operate machinery for activities such as backfilling excavations, vibrating or breaking rock or concrete, and perform preventative maintenance on vehicles and heavy equipment.
Drive heavy equipment and vehicles with adjustable attachments to sweep debris from paved surfaces, mow grass and weeds, remove snow and ice, and spread salt and sand.
Weld sections and components for track systems, such as switch points and frogs.
Grind ends of new or worn rails to attain smooth joints and clean, grade, or level ballast on railroad tracks.

Observe leveling indicator arms and adjust controls of machines that spread, shape, raise, level, or align track according to specifications, and operate track wrenches to tighten or loosen bolts at joints that hold rails together.
Inspect, clean, and repair drainage systems, bridges, tunnels, and other structures, and patrol assigned track sections to locate and report damaged or broken track.
Erect, install, or repair guardrails, road shoulders, highway markers, and warning signals, and set out signs and cones around work areas to divert traffic.
Drive trucks to transport crews and equipment to work sites, and haul and spread sand, gravel, and clay to fill washouts and repair road shoulders.
Perform roadside landscaping work, such as clearing weeds and brush, and planting and trimming trees.
Collect and record data associated with operations relating to construction and maintenance activities.
Maintain, calibrate, or repair equipment, and clean and adjust equipment using hand tools.
Inspect chains, belts, or scrolls for signs of wear.
Notify supervisors of needed repairs and examine materials to ensure conformance to established standards.
Conduct safety inspections and develop industry standards of product safety.
Compile, analyze, and interpret data related to occupational safety and health regulations.
Provide technical advice and guidance to organizations on health-related problems, and maintain liaisons with outside organizations for emergency responses.
Conduct or direct testing of air quality, noise, temperature, or radiation levels and review employee safety programs to determine their adequacy.
Review plans and specifications for construction to determine whether all safety requirements have been met, and recommend procedures for detection, prevention, and elimination of hazards.
Evaluate potential health hazards or damage that could occur from product misuse, and evaluate adequacy of actions taken to correct health inspection violations.

Investigate industrial accidents, injuries, or occupational diseases to determine causes and preventive measures, and report findings from accident investigations, facilities inspections, or environmental testing.
Conduct research to evaluate safety levels for products and consult with authorities to discuss safety regulations and recommend changes as necessary.
Conduct or coordinate worker training in areas such as safety laws and regulations, hazardous condition monitoring, and use of safety equipment.

Collect data for energy conservation analyses, evaluate energy efficiency of architectural, mechanical, or electrical plans or specifications, and prepare energy-related project reports or related documentation.
Monitor operations to identify any design, construction, or operation practices that violate safety regulations and initiate corrective actions in emergency situations.
Review or approve designs, calculations, or cost estimates related to construction projects, and document the design processes including objectives, issues, or outcomes.
Discuss construction project proposals with interested parties, such as vendors or contractors, and coordinate or supervise supplier or vendor work in the design, building, or testing phases.
Keep abreast of developments and changes in construction and safety through reading technical journals or by independent study and research.
Provide expert testimony in litigation cases regarding construction safety or practices related to industrial safety and processes.
Maintain and apply knowledge of current policies, regulations, and industrial processes related to construction and safety practices.
Evaluate fire department performance and laws affecting fire prevention or safety, and recommend preventive measures in handling potential hazards.
Document photonics system design processes and recommend processes for energy management in construction-related operations.
Investigate characteristics such as cost, performance, or process capability related to safety systems and measures in construction to improve efficiency.
Develop training materials and conduct training sessions on safety practices and equipment used in construction environments.
Ensure compliance with environmental testing regulations and safety standards in construction projects by overseeing site inspections and assessments.
Design or oversee construction and operation of safety and fire protection systems within construction environments.

Supervise, coordinate, or schedule the activities of construction or extractive workers and prepare proposals, budgets, and reports to ensure project completion within specifications, codes, and timelines.
Monitor work progress, safety, and compliance with specifications and regulations on construction sites, assessing contractor performance and coordinating inspections when necessary.
Provide technical assistance and support to installation teams for solar energy systems, overseeing training on construction methods, equipment operation, and safety procedures.
Plan and evaluate construction projects, consulting with other engineers and corporate personnel, and coordinate project activities to ensure efficiency and compliance.
Conduct site audits, assess material and worker requirements, and design project layouts to determine necessary equipment and ensure feasibility of construction activities.
Investigate accidents or injuries and prepare reports of findings to enhance safety and operational effectiveness in construction processes.
Continue education and training in construction engineering practices and standards, participating in industry conferences and staying updated on developments.
Analyse product failures, improvements, and performance data to enhance construction practices and project delivery, applying recommendations for better efficiency and quality assurance.
Review building plans, blueprints, and specifications to verify compliance and effectiveness of construction methods and identify necessary modifications.
Provide guidance on material selection and energy-efficient practices to minimize costs and enhance sustainability of construction projects.
Direct compliance activities associated with environmental standards to ensure adherence to safety regulations and best practices during construction.
Inspect and monitor work areas, examine tools and equipment, and provide employee safety training to prevent, detect, and correct unsafe conditions or violations of procedures and safety rules.
Confer with personnel such as management, engineering, quality control, or union representatives to coordinate work activities, resolve grievances, or identify resource needs.
Recommend or initiate personnel actions, such as hires, promotions, transfers, discharges, or disciplinary measures.
Perform skilled repair or maintenance operations using hand or power tools, hydraulic presses or shears, or welding equipment.
Develop, implement, or evaluate maintenance policies and procedures, and monitor tool and part inventories as well as the condition and maintenance of shops to ensure adequate working conditions.
Meet with vendors or suppliers to discuss products used in repair work.
Inspect, test, and measure completed work using hand tools or gauges to verify conformance to standards or repair requirements.
Compile operational or personnel records, such as time and production records, inventory data, or maintenance statistics.
Develop or implement electronic maintenance programs or computer information management systems, and design equipment configurations to meet personnel needs.
